title: Hubstaff Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Hubstaff** is a time-tracking + workforce-analytics platform exposing a REST API suite under `https://api.hubstaff.com/v2`. This connector — `HubstaffConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "hubstaff"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Hubstaff workspace:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Organizations | `/organizations` | List org workspaces the token belongs to |
| Users | `/users` + `/organizations/{id}/members` | Caller, by-id and per-org member queries |
| Teams | `/organizations/{id}/teams` | Teams inside an organization |
| Projects | `/organizations/{id}/projects`, `/projects/{id}` | List + get + create projects |
| Tasks | `/projects/{id}/tasks` | Tasks under a project |
| Activities | `/organizations/{id}/activities` | Tracked-time activity rows |
| Time Entries | `/organizations/{id}/time_entries` | Manual + tracked entries between dates |
| Daily Activities | `/organizations/{id}/activities/daily` | Per-user/day rolled-up summaries |
| Screenshots | `/organizations/{id}/screenshots` | Screenshot metadata (URL + time + user) |
| App Activity | `/organizations/{id}/application_activities` | App usage rows |
| URL Activity | `/organizations/{id}/url_activities` | URL/website usage rows |
| Notes | `/organizations/{id}/notes` | Notes attached to time entries |

The connector normalises projects + tasks + daily activities into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{connector_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts, honouring `Retry-After`), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::HubstaffHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Hubstaff Personal Access Token

1. Sign in to https://app.hubstaff.com.
2. Open **Settings → Developer → Personal Access Tokens** (or visit https://account.hubstaff.com/personal_access_tokens).
3. Click **Generate Personal Access Token**, name it (e.g. `shielva-acp`), and copy the value — Hubstaff shows it only once.
4. Find the numeric **Organization ID** of the workspace you want the connector scoped to (from the organization URL or `GET /organizations`).

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Hubstaff**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `access_token` (secret) | Personal Access Token from step 3 |
| `default_organization_id` (number, optional) | Numeric Organization ID — methods accept an override per call |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.hubstaff.com/v2` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `access_token` is present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `HubstaffHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which hits `GET /users/me` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 5xx/network → `OFFLINE + CONNECTED` with the underlying error in the `message` field.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Hubstaff REST API uses a **long-lived Personal Access Token (PAT)** sent as a bearer token. The connector treats the PAT as an opaque API key (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — no OAuth dance, no refresh exchange, no token rotation.

### Credential model

- `access_token` is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <token>`.
- `default_organization_id` is an install-time default used by `sync()` and convenience calls when the caller omits an `organization_id` argument.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `access_token`. Initialises `HubstaffHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Hubstaff endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a synthetic `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the PAT — there is no token exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /users/me` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None (the PAT lives in `self.config["access_token"]`). |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `access_token` | secret | yes | `self.access_token` | `Authorization: Bearer <...>` header value |
| `default_organization_id` | number | no | `self.default_organization_id` | Per-call default org id |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.hubstaff.com/v2`) | Override for sandbox / proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["access_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_HUBSTAFF_BASE = "https://api.hubstaff.com/v2"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `HubstaffConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::HubstaffHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `access_token`; initialise `HubstaffHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a synthetic TokenInfo with the PAT.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Calls `GET /users/me`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Hubstaff projects + their tasks + the org's recent daily-activity slice → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Identity & Organizations
children.content:
### `async get_current_user() -> Dict`
`GET /users/me`.

### `async get_user(user_id: int) -> Dict`
`GET /users/{id}`.

### `async list_organizations(*, page_start_id=None, page_limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations?page_limit={N}&page_start_id={S}`.

### `async list_users(organization_id, *, page_start_id=None, page_limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{organization_id}/members`.

### `async list_teams(organization_id) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{organization_id}/teams`.

children.title: Projects & Tasks
children.content:
### `async list_projects(organization_id, *, status="active", page_start_id=None, page_limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{organization_id}/projects?status={active|archived}`.

### `async get_project(project_id) -> Dict`
`GET /projects/{project_id}`.

### `async create_project(organization_id, name, description=None) -> Dict`
`POST /organizations/{organization_id}/projects` — body: `{project: {name, description?}}`.

### `async list_tasks(project_id, *, status="open", page_start_id=None, page_limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /projects/{project_id}/tasks?status={open|closed}`.

children.title: Activities, Time Entries, Daily Activities
children.content:
### `async list_activities(organization_id, *, date_start, date_stop, user_ids, project_ids, page_start_id, page_limit=100) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{organization_id}/activities`. user_ids / project_ids are CSV-encoded.

### `async list_time_entries(...) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{organization_id}/time_entries` with the same filter shape.

### `async list_daily_activities(...) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{organization_id}/activities/daily` — per-user/day rolled-up summaries.

children.title: Screenshots · Apps · URLs · Notes
children.content:
### `async list_screenshots(organization_id, *, date_start, date_stop, user_ids, project_ids, page_limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{organization_id}/screenshots`.

### `async list_apps(organization_id, *, date_start, date_stop, user_ids, page_limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{organization_id}/application_activities`.

### `async list_urls(organization_id, *, date_start, date_stop, user_ids, page_limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{organization_id}/url_activities`.

### `async list_notes(organization_id, *, date_start, date_stop, user_ids, page_limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{organization_id}/notes`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
HubstaffError                              # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── HubstaffAuthError                      # 401 / 403
├── HubstaffBadRequestError                # 400
├── HubstaffNotFoundError                  # 404
├── HubstaffConflictError                  # 409
├── HubstaffRateLimitError                 # 429 — retry_after_s
└── HubstaffServerError                    # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
HubstaffNetworkError = HubstaffServerError      # legacy name from older code
HubstaffNotFound     = HubstaffNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s), honouring `Retry-After`, retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Exponential backoff (same schedule), retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `HubstaffNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps them:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   CONNECTED)
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `access_token` blank | Re-paste the Personal Access Token in ACP. Hubstaff shows the token only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — PAT revoked, expired, or wrong workspace | Regenerate a PAT at https://account.hubstaff.com/personal_access_tokens and re-install. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — PAT lacks role/scope on the requested resource | The PAT inherits the user's permissions — confirm the Hubstaff user has Manager/Owner role on the organization. |
| `hubstaff.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Hubstaff per-minute rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff honouring `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `get_project(id)` | Project archived or belongs to a different org | Pass an `organization_id` you have access to OR re-list `list_projects` to confirm the id. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'HubstaffNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `HubstaffNetworkError = HubstaffServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| `Sync returned 0 documents` | `default_organization_id` is wrong OR org has no projects | Verify the numeric org id from `list_organizations()`; install a project and re-sync. |
